claims.claim: The Bank of Israel interest rate was cut to 3.75% on 2026-05-25 (previously 4.0% set 2026-01-05 from 4.25%). The Prime rate = BoI rate + 1.5% = 5.25% (the prior stale quote was Prime 5.5% at BoI 4.0%). BoI staff forecast points to ~3.5% by end-2026/early-2027.

2026 purchase tax (mas rechisha): first-apartment 0% bracket up to 1,978,745 NIS; investment/additional-property 8% up to 6,055,070 NIS and 10% above. Brackets are frozen (no CPI update) through 2027 by the Arrangements Law (next update 15.1.2028).
BoI Directive 329 LTV ceilings: first-time buyer 75%, upgrader 70%, investor 50%, foreign resident 50% (some banks 50-60%).

BoI Directive 329 requires at least 33.33% (one-third) of the mortgage in fixed-rate NIS tracks; variable + Prime combined up to 66.67% (two-thirds). The earlier separate Prime <= 33.33% cap was removed in the Directive 329 update of December 27, 2020 (effective January 2021). Mortgages must contain at least two tracks.
BoI does not legally cap payment-to-income (PTI) but flags >40% as high risk (raising the bank's capital requirement); banks run a stress test at Prime + 3 percentage points.
Bank of Israel publishes the current interest/Prime rate decisions; the BoI credit bureau (creditdata.org.il) provides a free annual credit report.
creditdata.org.il is the Bank of Israel credit bureau (BDI) free annual credit-report lookup.

The discounted-housing lottery runs under the Dira BeHanacha umbrella (gov.il); Mechir LaMishtaken is the original track and Mechir Matara the current flagship lottery; discounts typically 15-30% below market (national average around 22%).
The Ministry of Construction and Housing publishes a multi-track mortgage payment calculator that accounts for CPI linkage.

Illustrative example figures and typical ranges (not regulated constants), used in worked examples and guidance: example loan/property/income amounts 2,500,000 / 1,800,000 / 1,200,000 / 700,000 / 900,000 / 1,050,000 / 500,000 / 672,000 / 25,000 / 10,000 NIS; closing-cost ballparks attorney ~0.5% + VAT, appraiser 1,500-3,000 NIS, registration ~200 NIS, advisor fee 3,000-8,000 NIS; example track-mix splits 34% / 33% / 40% / 27% / 50% / 17% / 30% / 25%; example bank spreads and rates Prime-0.2% / 0.3% / 0.5% / 0.65% / 0.7%, fixed 3.8% / 4.0% / 4.25% / 4.5%; inflation scenarios 2% / 3% / 4%; example 72% LTV; refinancing rule-of-thumb save 0.3-0.5%; early-repayment penalty 0.5-2%; max term 30 years, age+term <= 75.
